D9 BiologyL3 ApplyReference

Coral Reef Fluorescence — UV-Blue → Green/Red Glow

corals express GFP-like proteins that absorb the abundant blue-violet light and fluoresce green/orange/red; night dives with blue lamps reveal a hidden fluorescent reef

Many reef corals carry GFP/RFP-family fluorescent proteins (FPs) in their tissues. Function debated: UV protection, prey luring, symbiont signalling. Mazel 2003 surveyed thousands of species and found ~30% express measurable FPs. Excite with blue (~470 nm); the reef glows green/orange/red.

Toggle white-light vs UV-blue night-dive view.
